title: Provisioning Clients as Code
description:
## Reproducible Provisioning

You must create **50 clients** across several environments, reproducibly, from a CI pipeline — and be able to recreate them identically if an environment is rebuilt.

What is the right approach?

options.text: Script the **Admin REST API** (or **kcadm.sh**) with an admin access token, checked into version control and run from CI
Open the Admin Console and click through creating each of the 50 clients by hand in every environment
Connect to Keycloak's database and `INSERT` the client rows directly with SQL
Create one client and tell all 50 applications to share its credentials

explanation: Reproducible, scripted provisioning is exactly what the **Admin REST API** and the **kcadm.sh** CLI are for: authenticate with an **admin access token** (e.g. as `admin-cli`), and create/update realms, clients, and users from code stored in git and executed by CI. (a) is correct. (b) is manual, slow, and error-prone — and it isn't reproducible across environments. (c) is **unsupported and dangerous**: Keycloak's schema is an internal detail, direct writes bypass validation/caching and can corrupt the realm. (d) is a security and operational anti-pattern — distinct applications should have distinct clients.

hints.title: Treat config as code
hints.body: Get a token from `/realms/master/protocol/openid-connect/token` (client_id=admin-cli), then POST to `/admin/realms/{realm}/clients`. kcadm.sh wraps this so your pipeline can declare clients declaratively.
